questions: Transaction/undo granularity: should a single resize inside a pinned shot group that shifts two clips be ONE undo step, or is two acceptable? The simpler implementation calls `onActionResizeEnd` twice (two history entries). Extending `onActionResizeEnd` to accept an array collapses it to one but touches `useClipResize.ts` and its consumers.

When the dragged edge would shrink the neighbor below `minDuration`, should the drag clamp (neighbor stops at minDuration, dragged edge halts) or should the neighbor be allowed to shrink past the visible minimum? The plan assumes clamp-the-drag.

For resizes where the right edge of the LAST clip or left edge of the FIRST clip is dragged, should we also skip snapping to OTHER group siblings (there are none adjacent on that free side)? The plan preserves existing snap behavior on free edges — confirm that's fine.

success_criteria.criterion: When a clip is inside a pinned shot group, right-clicking it shows only 'Update to current variant' and/or 'Dismiss reminder' (as applicable) and no Split Here / Delete Clip / Create Shot / Generate Video / existing shots list.
When a clip is inside a pinned shot group and has neither an available variant update nor a dismissable stale reminder, right-clicking does not open a context menu at all.
When a clip is NOT inside a pinned shot group, the context menu behaves exactly as before (no regressions).

Resizing the left edge of a middle-or-last clip inside a pinned shot group moves the previous clip's right edge to match, so the group remains contiguous and `positionedShotGroups` gap validation still renders the group overlay.
Resizing the right edge of a middle-or-first clip inside a pinned shot group moves the next clip's left edge to match.
Resizing the free outer edge (left of first clip, right of last clip) inside a pinned shot group does not shift any sibling.
Adjacent-clip auto-shift respects the existing minimum-duration constraint and does not collapse the neighbor below it.
Resizing clips OUTSIDE a pinned shot group is unchanged (no regression to standalone resize behavior, sibling snap still works).
All existing tests in `ClipAction.test.tsx` and `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` pass (excluding the pre-existing unrelated `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` failure called out in the brief).
New tests cover: gated menu with and without asset-state actions; auto-shift of previous-and-next neighbors on interior-edge resize; free-edge resize calling `onActionResizeEnd` once.
No files are touched outside `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/` (scope guard per brief).
No new toasts, no DB/migration/edge function changes, CLAUDE.md conventions followed (no hardcoded colors, shadcn patterns preserved).
A resize that shifts two clips produces a single undo step (may require extending `onActionResizeEnd` to accept an array).
`ClipAction.tsx` does not grow significantly in LOC beyond the minimum needed for the new gating (keep existing component structure).
Manual smoke: pin a 3-clip shot group, resize middle clip edges, verify label stays visible and neighbor edges follow; right-click each clip and confirm gating.

assumptions: `useClipResize.onActionResizeEnd` can be safely called twice in sequence for the primary and adjacent clip — the second call reads `dataRef.current` which reflects the first `applyEdit`'s committed state. If this turns out not to hold (e.g. `applyEdit` is async and dataRef lags), the array-batched signature will be required.
The existing `snapResize` call should be skipped only for the interior edge that has a group neighbor. Free outer edges still snap normally.
`shotGroups` prop on `TimelineCanvas` lists clips in left-to-right order within each group (consistent with `positionedShotGroups` iteration expecting `current.start >= previous.end`).

`isInPinnedShotGroup` suppression of the context menu also applies to the amber stale-variant badge click handler (`ClipAction.tsx:410`) for consistency — leaving it enabled would let users open the menu via the badge bypassing the gate.
The pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` test failure is genuinely unrelated; we will not fix or touch it.
`minDuration` for the adjacent clip is the same component-level `minDuration` already used in `updateResize` — no per-track override needed.
Proceeding with the two-call (two-undo-step) path by default; only switching to a batched array signature if tests or manual verification expose an undo/redo problem.
